items.title: Outback tourist town up in arms over proposed policing plan

children.content: When residents in Pine Creek call police in an emergency, the nearest 24-hour station is 100 kilometres away.

gallery.altText: A white brick wall with balck lettering that reads pine creek police station with a coat of arms above it

thumbnail.altText: A white brick wall with balck lettering that reads pine creek police station with a coat of arms above it

items.title: Telstra outage in remote NT community impacting Triple Zero access

children.content: Residents in the remote NT community of Kintore have had difficulty making Triple Zero calls during a six-day Telstra outage, which has also caused issues buying fuel.

image.altText: An aerial view of a town

items.title: NT working to repair flood damaged roads, with more than 1,000 potholes filled

children.content: More than 100 sections of road are closed or impassible following widespread flooding across the Northern Territory, with the cost of repairs set to rise with fuel prices.

image.altText: A wet, damaged road has chunks of bitumen missing.

items.title: Darwin's ambulance service fails overnight as 61 Triple Zero calls abandoned

children.content: Darwin's ambulance service has reached a "critical failure point" as 42 per cent of Triple Zero calls were abandoned overnight.

gallery.altText: Triple Zero emergency sign on St John NT ambulance

thumbnail.altText: Triple Zero emergency sign on St John NT ambulance

items.title: The unexpected type of man more women want to date

children.content: For the first time, more men than women are "marrying up" the education ladder, toppling a millennia-old tradition, an exclusive ABC analysis has revealed.

items.title: 'It's a nightmare': Complaints pile up over Booking.com grievances

children.content: Hundreds of complaints against Booking.com are piling up as customers and hosts allege they've lost money, their property has been trashed and no one is taking responsibility.

image.altText: A laptop on a table with the booking.com website, a coconut drink and two logos of a palm and umbrella.

items.title: 'A lover of life': Mal Leyland eyes off one final trip before dementia decline

children.content: Adventurer and filmmaker Mal Leyland, of Ask the Leyland Brothers fame, shares his private health battle. But the TV legend is determined to retrace the cross-country trip that made him famous 60 years ago.

image.altText: Man in his 80s wearing a collared shirt smiles, behind in post-graphic is a black and white photo of same man younger, shirtless

items.title: Family's living experiment reveals Australia's abysmal apartment design

children.content: In a quest for an affordable mortgage in our location of choice, my family decide to rent an apartment as a trial. Will high-rise living be a realistic alternative to a house as a place to bring up our child?

gallery.altText: Illustration of a living room with a girl in red clothes on a swing visible through the window and an apartment block behind

thumbnail.altText: Illustration of a living room with a girl in red clothes on a swing visible through the window and an apartment block behind

items.title: Cyclone Narelle destroys banana crops, fresh food growers hit hard

children.content: Fruit and vegetable producers in one of Western Australia's key food growing regions are counting the cost of Cyclone Narelle, with one banana grower reporting more than 80 per cent of his crop has been destroyed.

image.altText: A wide shot of a man wearing work uniform and walking through his banana plantation.

items.title: Is Labor trusting big tech too much?

children.content: As an artificial intelligence revolution approaches, the government is responding to a huge opportunity in data-centre development while balancing copyright concerns and workforce anxiety.

image.altText: Rowland stands in the prime minister's courtyard smiling.

items.title: 'This cash cow is going to die': Why Chinese student numbers are falling

children.content: Chinese students continue to provide the lion's hare of revenue for Australian universities. But experts warn that could soon change.

gallery.altText: A group of students wearing graduation gowns hold flowers.

thumbnail.altText: A group of students wearing graduation gowns hold flowers.

items.title: Australia has to attack misinformation about climate change, Senate report says

children.content: Australia needs to increase its efforts to fight back against misinformation about climate change, a Senate report says.

image.altText: The silhouette of an oil pumpjack in the foreground with a wind turbine in the background.

items.title: The 'one stop shop' providing a blueprint for Thriving Kids

children.content: While serious workforce questions surround the new Thriving Kids scheme, the organisers of an existing program in Victoria believe its model could be scaled up.

image.altText: Two toddlers interacting in a playgroup

items.title: How a 5,000-year-old garment went from being called 'traditional' to the Met Gala

children.content: For many South Asians, museums in the West have historically been places where their cultures were displayed as static artefacts. An exhibition like The Offbeat Sari has flipped that script, presenting South Asian creativity as innovative and globally influential.

gallery.altText: Priya Khanchandani wearing a cream coloured sari poses

thumbnail.altText: Priya Khanchandani wearing a cream coloured sari poses

items.title: Obongjayar is the genre-defying Nigerian star challenging stereotypes

children.content: The Nigerian-British musician brightens up dark times, whether it's his adventurous solo music or scene-stealing collabs with Fred again.. and Little Simz.

image.altText: Nigerian-British man with short dreads, wearing black sleeveless top and puffy, fashionable pants, crouches with arms out front.

items.title: Consider yourself a trivia nut? Have a crack at this quiz

children.content: We know you've come here looking for trivia glory but you're unlikely to find any today.

image.altText: Tom Gleeson on the set of Hard Quiz gesturing behind him with a serious expression.

items.title: Farmers face 'decision paralysis' as fuel costs threaten viability

children.content: The sudden rise in fuel costs is forcing farmers to make difficult decisions, such as laying off workers and selling equipment to keep their businesses running.

items.title: WA businesses say matter of weeks before fuel crisis sparks serious price rises

children.content: Country business owners say they have worked to absorb price increases, but will be forced to pass on costs to consumers if the supply of fuel to regional WA isn't stabilised.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a hand holding a full pint of beer below a beer tap after pouring it at a bar in a pub.

items.title: Public transport free for Victorians in April

children.content: The Victorian government says public transport will be free across the state in April in a bid to relieve cost-of-living pressure as fuel prices continue to climb.

items.title: Former AFL footballer asked how much he drank before he was allegedly raped

children.content: Daniel Hayes was 17 years old when he was allegedly raped by his coach while playing in an Australian Rules football talent league in Victoria.

image.altText: An Indigenous man in front of a wooden fence with mottled light over his face.

items.title: Gout beaten by Kennedy in epic Maurie Plant re-match as history repeats

children.content: In an identical performance to their titanic battle 12 months ago, Lachlan Kennedy edges out Gout Gout to win the men's 200 metres at the Maurie Plant Meet in Melbourne.

image.altText: Lachlan Kennedy defeats Gout Gout in the men's 200m at the 2026 Maurie Plant Meet.

items.title: From confectionery to boot polish, show bags were originally free sample bags

children.content: The "uniquely Australian" tradition of the show bag dates back more than a century, and shows no sign of dying out.

gallery.altText: Two boys wearing headresses share a drink beside their sample bags
A showbag filled with lollipops held up in a showbag van.
A woman holds a showbag in a showbag van.

A showbag filled with lollipops held up in a showbag van.
A woman holds a showbag in a showbag van.

items.title: Bracks rubbishes Kennett suggestion that Coalition, One Nation band together

children.content: Former premier Steve Bracks says One Nation teaming up with the Coalition would be a disaster for Victoria, and has hit out at Jeff Kennett for even suggesting it.

items.title: Locations of two new shark listening stations for Sydney revealed

children.content: Sydney's lower north shore and eastern suburbs are likely locations for two new shark listening devices following a spate of attacks earlier this year.

image.altText: A female bull shark in the water

items.title: 'Cool cat' of journalism captured decades of Australian history on 35mm film

children.content: Journalist and broadcaster Wayne Coolwell documented years of Indigenous culture with his 35mm camera. The photographs are going on showcase at the State Library.

image.altText: Black and white film photo of a man sitting at a radio mic in a booth

items.title: $350m funding boost to help 'prevent another stolen generation'

children.content: The team of case workers at Tharawal Aboriginal Corporation in south-west Sydney helping to prevent children from going into foster care is about to double in size.

gallery.altText: Tarnika Steward holds her young son as she looks at him smiling

thumbnail.altText: Tarnika Steward holds her young son as she looks at him smiling
